senses.gloss: اَلْأَحَد (al-ʔaḥad): ellipsis of يَوْم الْأَحَد (yawm al-ʔaḥad, “Sunday”, literally “day one”)

examples.text: لكنه عاد للعمل في صباح الأحد
examples.english: but he was back to work on Sunday morning

senses.gloss: a person; somebody, someone; anybody, anyone

examples.text: قُلْ هُوَ ٱللَّهُ أَحَدٌ ٱللَّهُ ٱلصَّمَدُ لَمْ يَلِدْ وَلَمْ يُولَدْ وَلَمْ يَكُن لَهُۥ كُفُوًا أَحَدٌ
examples.english: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “He is Allah—One ˹and Indivisible˺; Allah—the Sustainer ˹needed by all˺. He has never had offspring, nor was He born. And there is none comparable to Him.

examples.text: قُلْ هُوَ ٱللَّهُ أَحَدٌ ٱللَّهُ ٱلصَّمَدُ لَمْ يَلِدْ وَلَمْ يُولَدْ وَلَمْ يَكُن لَهُۥ كُفُوًا أَحَدٌ
examples.english: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “He is Allah—One ˹and Indivisible˺; Allah—the Sustainer ˹needed by all˺. He has never had offspring, nor was He born. And there is none comparable to Him.

senses.gloss: elative degree of حَادّ (ḥādd):; more acute, sharper, more cutting; most acute, sharpest, most cutting; very acute, sharp or cutting
elative degree of حَادّ (ḥādd):; more vehement; most vehement; very vehement
elative degree of حَدّ (ḥadd):; more violent, more passionate; most violent, most passionate; very violent, very passionate
a mountain north of Medina, Saudi Arabia

etymology.text:
Related to the root و ح د (w ḥ d) of وَاحِد (wāḥid, “one”), from Proto-Semitic *ʾaḥad-. Cognate with Hebrew אֶחָד (ekhád, “one”).

Elative of حَادّ (ḥādd, “sharp, vehement”) and حَدّ (ḥadd, “violent, passionate”).
